task_completion.description: Navigate to the interventions or treatments section in Ekylibre
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ekylibre web interface is open and displaying a page for creating a new intervention, treatment, or health care record.
The name 'Marguerite' or the ID '9001' is visible in the target/animal selection field of the intervention form.
The product name 'Curamycin' is entered or selected in the inputs/products field of the intervention form.

task_completion.description: Enter a quantity of 50ml for the product
task_completion.visual_evidence: The number '50' is visible in the quantity or dosage field associated with the Curamycin product.

task_completion.description: Categorize the intervention under sanitary/health care
task_completion.visual_evidence: A category such as 'Sanitary', 'Health care', or a similar medical classification is selected in the intervention type or category field.

task_completion.description: Save the veterinary treatment record successfully
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'Intervention was successfully created') is displayed, or the newly created record is visible in the list of interventions showing Marguerite and Curamycin.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Ekylibre graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Ekylibre application, not a terminal window sending API requests.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database or console commands to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing Rails console ('rails c') or SQL commands being used to manually insert the intervention record.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely filled out the form fields
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the progression of interacting with dropdowns and text inputs within the Ekylibre form, rather than just inspecting or modifying the HTML DOM directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target animal: 'Marguerite' (Work ID: '9001'). Product: 'Curamycin' (a veterinary antibiotic). Quantity: 50.0. The task requires creating an intervention record with these specific details in the Ekylibre system. The task description explicitly requires the intervention to be categorized under 'sanitary/health care', so visual evidence of this categorization should be checked in the UI.
